name: libjson-xs-perl
title: libjson-xs-perl
description:
module for manipulating JSON-formatted data (C/XS-accelerated)
JSON::XS is a module for manipulating data stored in the JavaScript Object
Notation (JSON) serialization format (defined in RFC 4627), which serves as
an alternative to XML.
.
This C/XS-accelerated JSON implementation confers significant performance
gains if available. After installing this package, any code using the JSON
module (see libjson-perl) will automagically benefit from this.

arguments.description: a Storable file (Storable has two incompatible formats)
arguments.raw: -file  a Storable file (Storable has two incompatible formats)

arguments.description: CBOR using extensions to make it smaller
arguments.raw: -packed  CBOR using extensions to make it smaller

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use json_xs because module for manipulating JSON-formatted data (C/XS-accelerated).
When the engagement is in the install phase, use json_xs as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `json_xs [-v] [-f inputformat] [-t outputformat]` as documented upstream for json_xs.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `json_xs -t none <isitreally.json` as documented upstream for json_xs.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `json_xs -f storable-file <file` as documented upstream for json_xs.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `json_xs -f storable-file -t yaml <file` as documented upstream for json_xs.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair json_xs with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `json_xs --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

Further invocations from upstream: `json_xs -f storable-file <file`; `json_xs -f storable-file -t yaml <file`. module for manipulating JSON-formatted data (C/XS-accelerated).
